ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general pizza class (FOODON_00002439); no specific Parma ham pizza subclass exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC covers pizza as a category; no specific Parma ham pizza code exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept for Parma ham pizza identified in the clinical terminology.

serving.natural_language_portion: One whole Parma ham pizza, approximately 300 g, as typically served at an Italian restaurant.

nutrient_source.title: Pizza with ham — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 21299) adapted with Parma ham composition from USDA Foundation Foods prosciutto entry (FDC ID 168194)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact USDA entry for Parma ham pizza exists. Values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy pizza with meat topping (NDB 21299) and cross-referenced with USDA Foundation Foods prosciutto (FDC ID 168194) to reflect the Parma ham component. Per-100g values represent a typical baked pizza with prosciutto topping, mozzarella, and tomato base. This is an estimated composite; confidence is medium.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — pizza, cheese, thin crust (GI ~60)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No GI value exists specifically for Parma ham pizza. The GI of 60 is taken from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for cheese pizza / thin-crust pizza with meat topping, which is the closest analogous food. Parma ham pizza shares the same wheat-flour dough base and similar topping density. GL per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (60 × 90.6) / 100 = 54.4, where carbohydrate per 300 g serving = 30.2 × 3 = 90.6 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content from Parma ham and cheese
hypertension.guidance: Parma ham pizza contains approximately 680 mg sodium per 100 g (approximately 2040 mg per whole pizza), which substantially exceeds the recommended single-meal sodium limit of 600–800 mg for individuals with hypertension; portion should be strictly limited to one or two slices and paired with low-sodium accompaniments.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Refined wheat dough with medium glycemic index (GI 60) and high glycemic load per serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: The refined wheat crust produces a medium GI of approximately 60 and a high glycemic load of approximately 54 per whole pizza; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit intake to one or two slices, choose thin-crust variants, and pair with a high-fibre salad to blunt the postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from cheese and cured ham, combined with high sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Each 100 g of Parma ham pizza provides approximately 4.2 g saturated fat and 680 mg sodium; regular consumption may contribute to elevated LDL cholesterol and blood pressure, so individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit portion size to one or two slices and choose preparations with reduced cheese and leaner cured meat.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Parma Ham Pizza is a gourmet Italian pizza topped with prosciutto di Parma — a dry-cured Italian ham produced in the Parma region of Emilia-Romagna under protected designation of origin (PDO) status. The pizza is built on a thin wheat-flour dough base spread with tomato sauce and melted mozzarella cheese, then finished with thinly sliced Parma ham, often added after baking to preserve the delicate flavour and silky texture of the cured meat. The result is a combination of savoury, slightly salty ham against a mild, creamy cheese base with a crisp, lightly charred crust. In Singapore, this pizza is served at Italian restaurants such as Pasta Fresca Da Salvatore and is priced at the premium end of the pizza market (approximately SGD 30). Nutritionally, per 100 g it provides approximately 266 kcal, 12.5 g protein, 10.8 g fat (of which 4.2 g is saturated), and 30.2 g carbohydrate. Sodium content is high at approximately 680 mg per 100 g due to the cured ham and cheese. The glycemic index is approximately 60 (medium), making it a moderate glycemic load food. Clinically, it is a concern for hypertension due to sodium, and requires portion control for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or cardiovascular disease. It is not suitable for vegetarians, vegans, or halal diets.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a Parma ham pizza?
Is Parma ham pizza high in sodium?
Can diabetics eat Parma ham pizza?
What is the GI of pizza?

confidence_reason: No exact USDA or primary regional database entry exists for Parma ham pizza as a composite dish. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy pizza with meat topping and USDA Foundation Foods prosciutto data. GI is estimated from the closest analogous pizza entry in the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium due to the composite estimation approach.
notes: Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted USDA source entries used for this composite estimate. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted USDA source entries. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted USDA source entries. All other -1 fields reflect genuine absence of data in the cited sources rather than confirmed zero values. No core macro is zero; all four core macros are positive and nutritionally plausible for a baked pizza with cured ham and cheese. GI of 60 is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database pizza entries; gi_category is medium per the Veritas threshold (56–69 inclusive). Glycemic load per serving = (60 × 90.6) / 100 = 54.4, where 90.6 g carbohydrate is based on a 300 g serving at 30.2 g carbohydrate per 100 g. This food is Italian cuisine; multilingual naming requirements apply to Italian cuisine (no mandatory Singaporean language set required). Chinese names are provided as supplementary local-market names given the Singapore field survey context. Parma ham is not halal-certified as it is a pork product.

improvement_actions_taken: Constructed composite nutrient profile from USDA SR Legacy pizza with meat topping and USDA Foundation Foods prosciutto entry.
Assigned GI 60 from University of Sydney GI Database analogous pizza entry with full documentation.

Confirmed no mandatory Singaporean language set required for Italian cuisine; Chinese names added as supplementary Singapore-market context.
